revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:隠岐の歴史]]
[[File:Oki islands in Shimane prefecture.png|thumb|Oki Islands in Shimane Prefecture]] '''History of Oki''' (隠岐の歴史) explains the {{ill|Oki Islands|en|Oki Islands|qid=Q483146|lt=Oki Islands|1=Oki Islands}}’s {{ill|History|en|History|qid=Q309|lt=History|1=History}}. The Oki Islands, located in the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, formed an autonomous region in ancient times as {{ill|Oki Province|en|Oki Province|qid=Q1063829|lt=Oki Province|1=Oki Province}}, and were also known as a nation of {{ill|Penal transportation|en|Penal transportation|qid=Q1770529|lt=Banishment|1=Penal transportation}}. In modern times, they became part of {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} and {{ill|Oki District, Shimane|en|Oki District, Shimane|qid=Q735735|lt=Oki District|1=Oki District, Shimane}}. After the war, {{ill|Depopulation|de|Entvölkerung|ja|過疎化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Depopulation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 過疎|1=Rural flight|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2608153}} has progressed. The {{ill|Sasaki Residence|de|Sasaki-Haus|ja|佐々木家住宅 (隠岐の島町)|lt=Sasaki Residence|lt_ja=佐々木家住宅|lt_de=Sasaki-Haus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Oki Local History Museum|de|Oki-Lokalmuseum|ja|隠岐郷土館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Local History Museum|1=Oki Kyōdo-kan Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24868915}} display exhibits concerning the history of Oki.
== History == === Prehistory ===
[[File:OKIKYOUDOKAN MARUKIBUNE IMG 8766.JPG|thumb|A dugout boat exhibited in front of the {{ill|Oki Local History Museum|de|Oki-Lokalmuseum|ja|隠岐郷土館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Local History Museum|1=Oki Kyōdo-kan Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24868915}}]]
In {{ill|Rear Oki|de|Hinter-Oki|ja|島後島|lt=Rear Oki|lt_ja=島後|lt_de=Hinter-Oki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rear Oki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 島後|1=Dōgojima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3623017}} of {{ill|Saigō, Shimane|en|Saigō, Shimane|qid=Q7400090|lt=Saigō Town|1=Saigō, Shimane}} (present-day {{ill|Okinoshima, Shimane|en|Okinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q1205039|lt=Oki Island Town|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}), the areas of Tsui and {{ill|Goka, Shimane|en|Goka, Shimane|qid=Q5578018|lt=Goka Village|1=Goka, Shimane}} (in the same region) produced {{ill|Obsidian|en|Obsidian|qid=Q53754|lt=Obsidian|1=Obsidian}} as a raw material for making stone tools. Around 5000 BC, early Jōmon sites such as the Miyabi site near Tsui in Saigō Town and the Nakamura Minato site near Hisami were discovered,<ref name="隠岐郷土館歴史年表">隠岐郷土館に掲示されている歴史年表</ref> and these sites are believed to represent evidence of stone tool production.
Oki’s obsidian—stronger than {{ill|Sanukite|de|Sanukait|ja|サヌカイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanukite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 讃岐岩|1=Sanukitoid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q843420}}—is widely distributed among Jōmon period sites throughout the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in Region|1=San'in region}}, extending east to the {{ill|Noto Peninsula|en|Noto Peninsula|qid=Q691151|lt=Noto Peninsula|1=Noto Peninsula}} and west to the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|de|Koreanische Halbinsel|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|qid=Q483134}}. In the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, wet‐rice cultivation was introduced to the island, and the Tsukinashi site appeared on the eastern bank of the lower Yao River in southern Rear Oki. Approximately 200 {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} are distributed across Oki, and along the lower Yao River lies the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in Oki—the {{ill|Heijinja Tomb|de|Heijinja-Grabhügel|ja|平神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijinja Tomb|1=Hei-jinja Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24876104}} (Heijinja), which measures 47 meters in total length with a horizontal stone chamber about 8 meters long.
=== Ancient Times ===
[[File:OKI BAREI IMG 9468.JPG|thumb|left|The station bell of the Oki Family Treasure Museum, as recorded in the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|qid=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}]]
Before the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|qid=Q570140|lt=Taika Reform|1=Taika Reform}}, a Regional Governor ({{ill|国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/国造|qq=}}) was established, and it is believed that the {{ill|Oki Family|de|Oki-Familie|ja|億岐家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 億岐氏|1=Oki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11387649}}, a family of {{ill|Shrine Chief Priest|de|Schreinsvorsteher|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453500}} at the {{ill|Tama Wakasu Shrine|de|Tama Wakasu-Schrein|ja|玉若酢命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Wakasu Shrine|1=Tamawakasu-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572773}}, served as the ruling family. In 646 (Taika 2), two station bells and the Oki Province seal were placed at the {{ill|Tama Wakasu Shrine|de|Tama Wakasu-Schrein|ja|玉若酢命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Wakasu Shrine|1=Tamawakasu-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572773}} in Saigō Town.<ref name="隠岐郷土館歴史年表"/> The exact date of Oki Province’s establishment is unknown, but it is thought to have existed from the time when provinces and districts were organized nationwide after the Taika Reform. Moreover, some {{ill|Mokkan|en|Mokkan|qid=Q4299994|lt=Mokkan|1=Mokkan}} from that period bear the inscription “隠伎国” (Oki Province), suggesting that this name may have originally been used.
The provincial government of Oki was located in the lower reaches of the Yao River in {{ill|Dōgojima|en|Dōgojima|qid=Q3623017|lt=Rear Oki|1=Dōgojima}}, which had been the largest central area in the archipelago since the Yayoi period; however, theories differ regarding its precise location, with proposals including placement on the Shimonishi plateau versus the Yao Plain. The exact sites of both the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|qid=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji|1=Provincial temple}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-nin Temple|de|Kokubun-Nin-Tempel|ja|国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-nin Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国分寺|1=Provincial temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1207757}} remain uncertain. In ancient times, Oki was considered one of the lower countries among the seven of the {{ill|San'indō|en|San'indō|qid=Q1323423|lt=San'in Circuit|1=San'indō}}, with travel from the capital set at 35 days outbound and 18 days on the return journey. The {{ill|Oki Family Treasure Museum|de|Oki-Familien-Schatzmuseum|ja|億岐家宝物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Family Treasure Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} exhibits a station bell designated as an Important Cultural Property.
The remote island of Oki in the Sea of Japan has, since ancient times, recorded diplomatic interactions with the [[:en:Balhae|Bohai]] and {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}. In {{ill|763|en|763|qid=Q30692|lt=Year 763|1=763}}, a Japanese mission returning from the Bohai Kingdom led by Heigunmushi Maro was shipwrecked in the Sea of Japan and washed ashore in Oki; in {{ill|825|en|825|qid=Q24624|lt=Year 825|1=825}}, 103 envoys including Takashōso from Bohai, and in {{ill|861|en|861|qid=Q23913|lt=Year 861|1=861}}, 105 envoys including Lee Gijeong arrived in Oki Province. As tensions between Japan and {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} increased, Oki was also affected; in {{ill|869|en|869|qid=Q23917|lt=Year 869|1=869}}, military archery instructors (crossbowmen) were stationed in Oki, and in 870 orders were issued for {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo Province}}, {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|qid=Q1053709|lt=Iwami|1=Iwami Province}}, and Oki to bolster defenses against Silla. In {{ill|AD 888|en|AD 888|qid=Q29956|lt=Year 888|1=AD 888}}, 35 Silla nationals were washed ashore in Oki, and in {{ill|943|en|943|qid=Q24179|lt=Year 943|1=943}}, seven Silla ships anchored, evidencing actual negotiations with Silla.
==== Counties and Districts in the Wamyō Ruijushō ====
According to the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|qid=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, Oki Province was divided into 4 districts and 12 townships: * {{ill|Chibu District|de|Chibu-Kreis|ja|知夫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu District|1=Chibu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584565}} – comprising Chiburi Island and Nishinoshima * Urago Township, Yura Township, Mita Township * {{ill|Ama District|de|Ama-Kreis|ja|海士郡|lt=Ama District|lt_ja=海部郡|lt_de=Ama-Kreis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama District|1=Ama district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558826}} – comprising Nakanojima * Fuse Township, Amabu Township, Satsukuri Township * {{ill|Shūkichi District|de|Shūkichi-Kreis|ja|周吉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūkichi District|1=Suki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10920245}} – in southern Rear Oki * Kamo Township, Amuka Township, Niino Township * {{ill|Onchi District|de|Onchi-Kreis|ja|穏地郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onchi District|1=Ochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597172}} – in northern Rear Oki * Tsuma Township, Kamuchi Township, Mura Township Onchi District was known in ancient times as Yaku-dō District.
==== Shikinaisha ====
[[File:Mizuwakasu-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|The primary shrine of Oki, {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|en|Mizuwakasu Shrine|qid=Q11549109|lt=Mizuwakasu Shrine|1=Mizuwakasu Shrine}}]]
According to the {{ill|Engishiki Shrine List|de|Engishiki-Schreinliste|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine List|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, Oki Province had 16 shrines (15 designated as {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}), of which the following four are considered {{ill|Famous Shrines|de|Berühmte Schreine|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Famous Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}: * {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|en|Mizuwakasu Shrine|qid=Q11549109|lt=Mizuwakasu Shrine|1=Mizuwakasu Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Onchi District|de|Onchi-Kreis|ja|穏地郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onchi District|1=Ochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597172}} in {{ill|Okinoshima, Shimane|en|Okinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q1205039|lt=Oki Island Town|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} (formerly part of Goka District) and recognized as Oki’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}. * {{ill|Ise-no-mikoto Shrine|de|Ise-no-mikoto-Schrein|ja|伊勢命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-no-mikoto Shrine|1=Isemikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378969}} – located in {{ill|Onchi District|de|Onchi-Kreis|ja|穏地郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onchi District|1=Ochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597172}} (in the area of Kumii in {{ill|Okinoshima, Shimane|en|Okinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q1205039|lt=Oki Island Town|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}) * {{ill|Yurahime Shrine|de|Yurahime-Schrein|ja|由良比女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yurahime Shrine|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Chibu District|de|Chibu-Kreis|ja|知夫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu District|1=Chibu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584565}} (in Urago, Nishinoshima Town), relocated from Chiburi Island * {{ill|Ujugah-no-mikoto Shrine|de|Ujugah-no-mikoto-Schrein|ja|宇受賀命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujugah-no-mikoto Shrine|1=Ujika Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449116}} – located in {{ill|Ama District|de|Ama-Kreis|ja|海士郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama District|1=Ama district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558826}} (in Ujugaha, {{ill|Ama, Shimane|en|Ama, Shimane|qid=Q685865|lt=Ama Town|1=Ama, Shimane}}) The abundance of Famous Shrines in this remote region is attributed to its proximity to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, which led the imperial court to bestow patronage for national defense reasons. In addition, the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Tama Wakasu Shrine|de|Tama Wakasu-Schrein|ja|玉若酢命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Wakasu Shrine|1=Tamawakasu-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572773}} in {{ill|Shūkichi District|de|Shūkichi-Kreis|ja|周吉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūkichi District|1=Suki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10920245}} (in the Shimose area of Nishinoshima Town) is located at the provincial government site and is regarded as the {{ill|Sōsha|de|Sōsha|ja|惣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 総社|1=Sōja shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1107129}} of Oki.
=== Medieval Period ===
[[File:海士町後鳥羽天皇御火葬塚.jpg|thumb|“The Cremation Mound of Emperor Go-Toba”]]
In the 4th year of the {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|qid=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū Era|1=Kenkyū}} ({{ill|1193|en|1193|qid=Q19741|lt=Year 1193|1=1193}}), it is recorded in the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|qid=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}} that {{ill|Sasaki Sadatsuna|de|Sasaki Sadatsuna|ja|佐々木定綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasaki Sadatsuna|1=Sasaki Sadatsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382187}} was appointed to the position of Jito for all of Oki. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|qid=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū Era|1=Jōkyū}} ({{ill|1221|en|1221|qid=Q5393|lt=Year 1221|1=1221}}), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|qid=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}} was exiled to the {{ill|Ama District|de|Ama-Kreis|ja|海士郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama District|1=Ama district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558826}}, where he spent 19 years in confinement; in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Genkō (1331–1334)|en|Genkō (1331–1334)|qid=Q1194556|lt=Genkō Era|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} ({{ill|1332|en|1332|qid=Q6001|lt=Year 1332|1=1332}}), {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} was likewise exiled. There are theories that the site of the emperor’s exile was either the Kokubun-ji in {{ill|Dōgojima|en|Dōgojima|qid=Q3623017|lt=Rear Oki|1=Dōgojima}} or the Kuroki Imperial Residence in {{ill|Front Oki|de|Vorder-Oki|ja|島前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Front Oki|1=Dōzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11476216}}, and although no definitive conclusion has been reached, the emperor eventually escaped.
=== Early Modern Period ===
[[File:Precincts of Takuhi jinja.JPG|thumb|left|In Nishinoshima, the {{ill|Yakabi Shrine|de|Yakabi-Schrein|ja|焼火神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakabi Shrine|1=Takuhi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11567668}}]]
In the 5th year of the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō Era|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|1600|en|1600|qid=Q6720|lt=Year 1600|1=1600}}), {{ill|Horio Yoshiharu|en|Horio Yoshiharu|qid=Q8190645|lt=Horio Yoshiharu|1=Horio Yoshiharu}} became the governor of Izumo and Oki, but from the 11th year of the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kanei Era|1=Kan'ei}} ({{ill|1634|en|1634|qid=Q6872|lt=Year 1634|1=1634}}) he was replaced by {{ill|Kyōgoku Tadataka|en|Kyōgoku Tadataka|qid=Q11374423|lt=Kyogoku Tadataka|1=Kyōgoku Tadataka}}, a descendant of the Muromachi-period guardians of Izumo and Oki. In the 15th year of the Kanei Era ({{ill|1638|en|1638|qid=Q6893|lt=Year 1638|1=1638}}), {{ill|Matsudaira Naomasa|de|Matsudaira Naomasa|ja|松平直政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomasa|1=Matsudaira Naomasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5324802}} entered Izumo, and henceforth Oki became a territory under direct shogunal control (administered as part of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|qid=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}). Entrusted by the shogunate with governance, the Matsue Domain established a regional office in Saigō, delegated administrative oversight to district heads, and dispatched magistrates to both {{ill|Front Oki|de|Vorder-Oki|ja|島前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Front Oki|1=Dōzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11476216}} and {{ill|Dōgojima|en|Dōgojima|qid=Q3623017|lt=Rear Oki|1=Dōgojima}}. Although the total assessed kokudaka of Oki was recorded as 18,000 koku, in reality it amounted to about 12,000 koku.
[[File:OKI SASAKIKE IMG 8697.JPG|thumb|The Important Cultural Property, the Sasaki Residence]]
The port of {{ill|Saigō, Shimane|en|Saigō, Shimane|qid=Q7400090|lt=Saigō Town|1=Saigō, Shimane}} in Rear Oki began to flourish from the {{ill|18th century|en|18th century|qid=Q7015|lt=18th century|1=18th century}} as a waiting and resupply port for the {{ill|Kitamaebune|en|Kitamaebune|qid=Q3440099|lt=Kitamaebune|1=Kitamaebune}}. This was because Rear Oki lay along the maritime route that connected {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|qid=Q1047671|lt=Noto|1=Noto Province}} directly to either {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|qid=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki|1=Shimonoseki}} or {{ill|Hakata (disambiguation)|en|Hakata (disambiguation)|qid=Q1208762|lt=Hakata|1=Hakata (disambiguation)}}, a route used for open-sea voyages. At Saigō Port, wholesalers operated as both a {{ill|Boat Inn|de|Bootsherberge|ja|舟宿|lt=Boat Inn|lt_ja=船宿|lt_de=Bootsherberge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat Inn|1=Boat Inn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56351170}} and as independent operators of {{ill|Round-trip Shipping|de|Rundschifffahrt|ja|廻船|lt=Round-trip Shipping|lt_ja=回船業|lt_de=Rundschifffahrt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round-trip Shipping|1=kaisen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486879}}. During this period, the {{ill|Yakabi Shrine|de|Yakabi-Schrein|ja|焼火神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakabi Shrine|1=Takuhi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11567668}} garnered the devotion of Kitamaebune crews as the deity of maritime safety. Since the Kitamaebune served to supply iron and rice from Yasugi throughout the Sea of Japan, the shipping route connecting Oki, Mihonoseki, and Yasugi persisted; even after its discontinuation, a branch of the Oki Steamship Company—carrying on the legacy of the Kitamaebune—continued to operate in Yasugi. The Sasaki Residence, built in {{ill|1836|en|1836|qid=Q7601|lt=Year 1836|1=1836}} (7th year of Tenpō), is recognized as the oldest wooden residence in Oki.
In the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=Year 1868|1=1868}}), the {{ill|Oki Disturbance|de|Oki-Aufruhr|ja|隠岐騒動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Disturbance|1=Oki Disturbance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659056}} erupted, during which a coalition of priests and village heads expelled the Matsue Domain’s head of Oki District, declaring Oki to be imperial property under the {{ill|Restoration of Imperial Rule|de|Wiederherstellung der kaiserlichen Herrschaft|ja|王政復古 (日本)|lt=Restoration of Imperial Rule|lt_ja=王政復古|lt_de=Wiederherstellung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Restoration of Imperial Rule|1=restoration of imperial rule|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q835568}} and governing autonomously. Although the Matsue Domain sent troops and temporarily recaptured Oki, they soon withdrew their forces through mediation by the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|qid=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}, allowing self-governance to be restored. The new Meiji government initially placed Oki under the administration of {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, and in the 2nd year of Meiji ({{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=Year 1869|1=1869}}) established an independent {{ill|Oki Prefecture|de|Oki-Präfektur|ja|隠岐県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浜田県|1=Hamada Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557991}} from February to August; it was later merged into the {{ill|Ōmori Prefecture|de|Ōmori-Präfektur|ja|大森県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浜田県|1=Hamada Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557991}}, which had its origins in the shogunate’s territory of {{ill|Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine|en|Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine|qid=Q174139|lt=Iwami Ginzan|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}}. Due to indecision regarding policy, the Oki region was repeatedly transferred between {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}} and {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}, and in the 9th year of Meiji ({{ill|1876|en|1876|qid=Q7766|lt=Year 1876|1=1876}}) it was finally incorporated into {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}.
=== Modern Era ===
[[File:Oki Kyodo-kan.jpg|thumb|left|The former Oki Subprefecture building (now the {{ill|Oki Local History Museum|de|Oki-Lokalmuseum|ja|隠岐郷土館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Local History Museum|1=Oki Kyōdo-kan Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24868915}})]]
After the Oki Islands were incorporated into Shimane Prefecture, they had been divided into the four districts—{{ill|Ama District|de|Ama-Kreis|ja|海士郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama District|1=Ama district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558826}}, {{ill|Chibu District|de|Chibu-Kreis|ja|知夫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu District|1=Chibu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584565}}, {{ill|Shūkichi District|de|Shūkichi-Kreis|ja|周吉郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūkichi District|1=Suki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10920245}}, and {{ill|Onchi District|de|Onchi-Kreis|ja|穏地郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onchi District|1=Ochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597172}}—that had existed since ancient times. In 1888 (Meiji 21), the {{ill|Shimane Prefectural Government|de|Regierung der Präfektur Shimane|ja|島根県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefectural Government|1=Shimane prefectural government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20044826}} abolished these districts and established the Oki Island Office, with the island governor administering the area. In 1904 (Meiji 37), in Rear Oki one town and seven villages (including {{ill|Saigō, Shimane|en|Saigō, Shimane|qid=Q7400090|lt=Saigō Town|1=Saigō, Shimane}}) and in Front Oki four villages were established. On February 15, 1905 (Meiji 38), {{ill|Takeshima (disambiguation)|en|Takeshima (disambiguation)|qid=Q1178087|lt=Takeshima|1=Takeshima (disambiguation)}} was confirmed as Japanese territory, later becoming part of {{ill|Goka, Shimane|en|Goka, Shimane|qid=Q5578018|lt=Goka Village|1=Goka, Shimane}}. This occurred because when {{ill|Nakai Yosaburō|de|Nakai Yosaburō|ja|中井養三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakai Yosaburō|1=Yōzaburō Nakai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11362963}} requested the government to lease Ryangko Island for seal and sea lion fishing, the government was unable to secure confirmation of the island’s affiliation. Ryangko Island was subsequently named Takeshima and came under the jurisdiction of the Oki Island governor. In 1925 (Taishō 14), the Oki Island Office became the {{ill|Oki Subprefecture|de|Oki-Unterpräfektur|ja|隠岐支庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Subprefecture|1=Oki Subprefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659048}}.
[[File:Oki Airport Aerial photograph.2015.jpg|thumb|The {{ill|Oki Airport|en|Oki Airport|qid=Q1148447|lt=Oki Airport|1=Oki Airport}}, opened in 1965]]
During the war, as Oki had no military bases, it was spared from wartime damage and instead became a refuge for evacuees. After the war, the population increased due to repatriates from overseas; however, with the onset of Japan’s {{ill|Japanese economic miracle|en|Japanese economic miracle|qid=Q1059474|lt=High Economic Growth|1=Japanese economic miracle}}, migration to urban centers accelerated, and depopulation gradually took hold in the Oki Islands. In 1953 (Shōwa 28), the {{ill|Islands Promotion Act|de|Inselförderungsgesetz|ja|離島振興法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Islands Promotion Act|1=Remote Islands Development Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659492}} was enacted, and in 1970 (Shōwa 45), emergency measures for depopulated regions were implemented, spurring infrastructure improvements. In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the area was designated as part of the {{ill|Daisen-Oki National Park|en|Daisen-Oki National Park|qid=Q1155784|lt=Daisen Oki National Park|1=Daisen-Oki National Park}}, and in 1965 (Shōwa 40) the {{ill|Oki Airport|en|Oki Airport|qid=Q1148447|lt=Oki Airport|1=Oki Airport}} was constructed in {{ill|Saigō, Shimane|en|Saigō, Shimane|qid=Q7400090|lt=Saigō Town|1=Saigō, Shimane}}, establishing it as a hub for tourism in Oki. In 1969 (Shōwa 44), the four former districts of Oki were merged to form the {{ill|Oki District, Shimane|en|Oki District, Shimane|qid=Q735735|lt=Oki District|1=Oki District, Shimane}}.
[[File:Okinosshimacho yakuba.JPG|thumb|{{ill|Okinoshima, Shimane|en|Okinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q1205039|lt=Oki Island Town|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} Office]] [[File:西ノ島町役場.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Nishinoshima, Shimane|en|Nishinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q543193|lt=Nishinoshima Town|1=Nishinoshima, Shimane}} Office]] [[File:Ama Town Hall ac.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Ama, Shimane|en|Ama, Shimane|qid=Q685865|lt=Ama Town|1=Ama, Shimane}} Office]] [[File:Chibu Village Office.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|Chibu, Shimane|qid=Q1204968|lt=Chibu Village|1=Chibu, Shimane}} Office]]
== Evolution of Municipalities == [[File:Oki Islands Relief Map, SRTM.jpg|thumb|Relief map of Oki]]
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small" |- ! colspan=5| Evolution of Municipalities in the Front Oki Region |- ! Region !! 1904–1946 !! 1946–1957 !! 1957–1969 !! 1969–present |- | rowspan=4| '''{{ill|Front Oki|de|Vorder-Oki|ja|島前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Front Oki|1=Dōzen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11476216}}''' || {{ill|Urago Village|de|Urago-Dorf|ja|浦郷町|lt=Urago Village|lt_ja=浦郷村|lt_de=Urago-Dorf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urago Village|1=Uragō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558363}} || November 3, 1946<br>Town system implemented as {{ill|Urago Town|de|Urago-Stadt|ja|浦郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urago Town|1=Uragō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558363}} || rowspan=2| February 11, 1957<br>Merger forming {{ill|Nishinoshima, Shimane|en|Nishinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q543193|lt=Nishinoshima Town|1=Nishinoshima, Shimane}} || rowspan=2| Nishinoshima Town |- || {{ill|Kuroki Village|de|Kuroki-Dorf|ja|黒木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroki Village|1=Kurogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678326}} || {{ill|Kuroki Village|de|Kuroki-Dorf|ja|黒木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroki Village|1=Kurogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678326}} |- || {{ill|Ama, Shimane|en|Ama, Shimane|qid=Q685865|lt=Ama Village|1=Ama, Shimane}} || Ama Village || January 1, 1969<br>Town system implemented as {{ill|Ama, Shimane|en|Ama, Shimane|qid=Q685865|lt=Ama Town|1=Ama, Shimane}} |- || {{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|Chibu, Shimane|qid=Q1204968|lt=Chibu Village|1=Chibu, Shimane}} || Chibu Village || Chibu Village |}
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small" |- ! colspan=7| Evolution of Municipalities in the Rear Oki Region |- ! Region !! 1904–1954 !! 1954–1960 !! 1960–2004 !! 2004–present |- | rowspan=8| '''{{ill|Dōgojima|en|Dōgojima|qid=Q3623017|lt=Rear Oki|1=Dōgojima}}''' || {{ill|Saigō, Shimane|en|Saigō, Shimane|qid=Q7400090|lt=Saigō Town|1=Saigō, Shimane}} || rowspan=4| July 1, 1954<br>Merger forming Saigō Town || rowspan=5| November 1, 1960<br>Merger forming Saigō Town || rowspan=8| October 1, 2004<br>Merger forming {{ill|Okinoshima, Shimane|en|Okinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q1205039|lt=Oki Island Town|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} |- || {{ill|Tōgō Village|de|Tōgō-Dorf|ja|東郷村 (島根県)|lt=Tōgō Village|lt_ja=東郷村|lt_de=Tōgō-Dorf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Village|1=Tōgō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653708}} |- || {{ill|Chūjō Village|de|Chūjō-Dorf|ja|中条村 (島根県)|lt=Chūjō Village|lt_ja=中条村|lt_de=Chūjō-Dorf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūjō Village|1=Nakasuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653886}} |- || {{ill|Iso Village|de|Iso-Dorf|ja|磯村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iso Village|1=Iso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20043123}} |- || {{ill|Nakamura Village|de|Nakamura-Dorf|ja|中村 (島根県)|lt=Nakamura Village|lt_ja=中村|lt_de=Nakamura-Dorf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Village|1=Naka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365155}} || Nakamura |- || {{ill|Fuse, Shimane|en|Fuse, Shimane|qid=Q5509971|lt=Fuse Village|1=Fuse, Shimane}} || Fuse Village || Fuse Village |- || {{ill|Tsuma, Shimane|en|Tsuma, Shimane|qid=Q11643464|lt=Toman Village|1=Tsuma, Shimane}} || Toman Village || Toman Village |- || {{ill|Goka, Shimane|en|Goka, Shimane|qid=Q5578018|lt=Goka Village|1=Goka, Shimane}} || Goka Village || Goka Village |}
<gallery> File:Okinosshimacho yakuba.JPG|{{ill|Okinoshima, Shimane|en|Okinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q1205039|lt=Oki Island Town|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} Office File:西ノ島町役場.jpg|{{ill|Nishinoshima, Shimane|en|Nishinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q543193|lt=Nishinoshima Town|1=Nishinoshima, Shimane}} Office File:Ama Town Hall ac.jpg|{{ill|Ama, Shimane|en|Ama, Shimane|qid=Q685865|lt=Ama Town|1=Ama, Shimane}} Office File:Chibu Village Office.jpg|{{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|Chibu, Shimane|qid=Q1204968|lt=Chibu Village|1=Chibu, Shimane}} Office </gallery>
== Takeshima == [[File:Shimane Goko-vill Takeshima.png|thumb|Takeshima]]
{{ill|Takeshima (disambiguation)|en|Takeshima (disambiguation)|qid=Q1178087|lt=Takeshima|1=Takeshima (disambiguation)}} is a reef (total area 230,967 m²) located approximately 157 km northwest of Oki Island. On February 22, 1905 (Meiji 38), a cabinet decision designated it under the jurisdiction of the Oki Island governor of {{ill|Saigō, Shimane|en|Saigō, Shimane|qid=Q7400090|lt=Saigō Town|1=Saigō, Shimane}}, and it was recorded in Shimane Prefecture’s public land register on May 17 of the same year. During the war, it was used as naval land under the {{ill|Maizuru Naval District|en|Maizuru Naval District|qid=Q2688131|lt=Maizuru Naval Base|1=Maizuru Naval District}}. Since 1953 (Shōwa 28), it has been under the {{ill|Effective Control|de|Effektive Kontrolle|ja|実効支配|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Effective Control|1=effective control|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379289}} of the {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|qid=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}}, although according to the Japanese government it is Japanese {{ill|State ownership|en|State ownership|qid=Q2396633|lt=State-owned Property|1=State ownership}} under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of finance|en|Ministry of finance|qid=Q15711797|lt=Ministry of Finance|1=Ministry of finance}}’s Fiscal Bureau. The {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|qid=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard|1=Japan Coast Guard}} has established the Oki Maritime Safety Office in {{ill|Okinoshima, Shimane|en|Okinoshima, Shimane|qid=Q1205039|lt=Oki Island Town|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} to secure the surrounding waters.
